search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG106 echocardiography result review heart failure
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic valve or heart-failure diagnosis and treatment
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Interpretation requires the actual report or images, technical quality, indication, comparison, and qualified clinician review.
No diagnosis, severity grade, treatment, referral, or follow-up interval was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with an echocardiogram being reviewed; source applicability depends on heart-failure or valve-disease context.
setting_applicability: Primary, outpatient, and specialist result review; acute instability requires direct assessment.
UAE_applicability: International NICE evidence requires UAE echocardiography reporting, cardiology, referral, and facility-policy adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG106 and NG208 sources were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: Both reviewed NICE guidelines remain current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE heart-failure section supports documenting the echocardiogram source and date, reason for review, symptom and examination context, and clinician review of cardiac structure or function when heart failure is suspected; it does not create an interpretation or diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The NICE valve section confirms echocardiography relevance for clinician-assessed murmur and valve-disease context; it is conditional and does not classify a valve lesion.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports murmur nature, family and medical history, symptoms, peripheral signs, abnormal ECG, and clinician-led echocardiography review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The review section supports documenting clinician explanation, an entered plan, safety-net discussion, and arranged follow-up without generating management.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

unresolved_source_gaps: Indication, date, modality, image quality, chambers, function, valves, pressures, effusion, measurements, comparison, formal interpretation, diagnosis, severity, urgency, treatment, referral, communication, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
